test_cases.title: Valid user sees inventory after login
test_cases.description: Happy path: a registered, non-locked user signs in to Saucedemo with correct credentials and reaches the inventory list.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: The AC is about the user journey through the UI — submit the form, land on the inventory page. The journey crosses page boundaries; an API check alone would not validate the redirect and inventory render. Also the positive baseline against which TC-002 diverges.
preconditions: A non-locked Saucedemo user exists (standard_user / secret_sauce).
expected_results: URL changes to a path ending in /inventory.html.
At least one inventory item is visible on the page.

test_cases.title: Invalid password is rejected and no session is created
test_cases.description: Negative case: a wrong password keeps the user on the login page, shows the error banner, and sets no session cookie.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: The AC names UI feedback (remain on login page, see error) plus the no-session guarantee. The UI feedback is an E2E assertion; the no-session part is checked via the session-username cookie absence, the strongest signal available on this backend-less UI.

steps.action: Fill the Password field with a wrong value.

expected_results: URL remains on the login page (path is '/').
The error banner is visible: 'Epic sadface: Username and password do not match any user in this service'.

test_cases.title: Create user returns 201 with id and createdAt
test_cases.description: POST /users with a valid name and job creates a user and returns 201 with an id and a createdAt timestamp.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: Server-side contract: a POST returning a specific status and response shape. No UI to exercise; an API test verifies the contract deterministically. Status-code and data-shape checks belong at the API level per docs/automation-decision-model.md.
preconditions: The API base URL is configured via the Postman environment (reqres.in).

steps.action: POST to /users with a JSON body.

test_cases.title: Register with missing password is rejected
test_cases.description: POST /register with an email but no password returns 400 with an error message (reqres.in's documented missing-field failure mode).
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: Negative validation at the API contract level: a missing required field must be rejected with 400 and a clear error. Negative API validation is a primary use of automate_api per docs/automation-decision-model.md.
preconditions: The API base URL is configured via the Postman environment (reqres.in).

steps.action: POST to /register with only an email field.

expected_results: Response body contains an 'error' field mentioning the missing password.
